name: Sweet-And-Sour Chicken
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Rice wine or dry sherry
3 oz Canned lychees, drained -OR- 1 Fresh orange in segments
recipeInstructions:
1. CUT THE CHICKEN INTO 1-INCH CUBES. Place the cubes in a bowl with the rice wine or sherry, 1 tablespoon of light soy sauce, and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Marinate for 20 minutes.
2. Meanwhile, chop the green and red peppers into 1-inch squares. Peel and cut the carrots and scallions into 1-inch chunks.
3. Bring a pot of water to a boil and blanch the carrots for 4 minutes. Drain and set aside.
4. Mix the egg and cornstarch in a bowl until well blended into a batter.
5. Remove the chicken cubes from the marinade, coat each piece with the batter, and heat the oil in a deep-fat fryer or large wok until it is almost smoking.
6. Using a slotted spoon, remove the chicken pieces from the batter and deep-fry them. Drain on paper towels.
7. TO MAKE THE SAUCE: In a large saucepan, combine the chicken stock, soy sauce, salt, vinegar, sugar, and tomato paste. Bring to a boil.
8. Add the vegetables (excluding lychees or oranges) and stir well.
9. Blend the cornstarch and water in a small bowl. Stir this mixture into the sauce and return it to a boil.
10. Reduce heat to a simmer, add the lychees or oranges and chicken cubes, mix well, and serve immediately.
